{{Quote|This was the ''Keeper''… and its task was to rebuild and ready the war-torn world for the day its ''people'' would come back.|The Keeper's history lesson|<ref name="TKS" />}}
'''The Keeper''' was a machine with an [[Artificial intelligence/Legends|artificial intelligence]] located on [[Keeper's World]]. It was accompanied by four [[Android/Legends|android]] children. Built by a [[Keeper's makers|non-violent civilization]] to preserve their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] in a [[Time/Legends|time]] of [[Unidentified war|war]], the Keeper drew its power from its home [[Planet/Legends|planet's]] core, which allowed it to survive long after the people had fled. The Keeper was installed to restore the world to its former status for its makers, so that when they would return [[Standard year/Legends|years]] later, the planet would be suitable for them to live on.

Shortly after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] agents [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] landed on the Keeper's planet seeking shelter from an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]]. Battling their way through the wilderness with the aid of their two [[Droid/Legends|droids]], [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] and [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]], they stumbled upon the [[Keeper's Temple]], and in turn met the Keeper's four android children. The Keeper was convinced that the Rebels were of good faith, and decided to help them by forcing the Imperial [[Trooper/Legends|troops]] that had landed to flee offworld. Skywalker and Organa then left the Keeper in peace to continue her task of building a civilization.

==Biography==
===Creation===
At some stage prior to the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial era]], the [[Keeper's makers|people]] of [[Keeper's World]] fled from their homes to escape impending destruction in a [[Unidentified war|galaxy-wide war]]. However, the people were fond of their homeworld, and wished to restore it to its former state. They thus built the Keeper; a gargantuan machine which drew its power from the [[Planet/Legends|planet's]] core. They tasked the Keeper with reshaping its creators' world so that the planet would be as it was by the [[Time/Legends|time]] of their return.<ref name="TKS" />

===The Children===
[[File:KeeperChildren.jpg|thumb|left|170px|The Keeper creates her Children.]]
The Keeper had been [[Programmer/Legends|programmed]] with a [[Parent/Legends|mother's]] instinct, and since its work lasted many centuries, it naturally grew lonely. To counter this, it created four android "Children" to provide company. These Children resembled the youths of the Keeper's civilization, save for different [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], and they were imbued with abilities that would allow each one of the four to control a separate element of nature: [[Fire (individual)|fire]], [[Earth (droid)|earth]], [[Wind (android)|wind]], and [[Rain (droid)|water]]. The children were tasked with aiding the Keeper in reshaping the world—by using each of their powers to their full extent, the Keeper hoped they would be able to recreate a fully functioning ecosystem.<ref name="TKS" />

When the Keeper and her children recreated the jungles of their world, they also created gargantuan monsters that towered over the [[Tree/Legends|trees]]. The Keeper and the Children used this to their advantage, and employed the creatures as security for their temple. The Keeper maintained the hostile appearance of the planet to ward off any would be intruders, invaders, or any new settlers.<ref name="TKS" />

===Galactic Civil War===
Sometime in [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]]—agents of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]—set out from a [[Great Temple/Legends|rebel base]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] to make contact with the second major Rebel base, located in the [[Akuria system/Legends|Akuria system]]. Due to a poor repair job performed on R2-D2 following the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], the [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] caused their [[Starship/Legends|starship]] to prematurely exit [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], and, by coincidence, in the middle of an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] fleet.<ref name="UPSWS1">{{StoryCite|story=Untitled Pizzazz 1 comic|book=Pizzazz 1|sformatted=Untitled comic}}</ref> However, due to Skywalker's [[The Force/Legends|Force]]-enhanced [[Pilot/Legends|piloting]] skills, the Rebels were able to temporarily escape capture. His evasive maneuvers caused the Rebel [[Transport/Legends|transport]] to become caught in the [[Gravity/Legends|gravitational]] pull of Keeper's World, where it was forced to land.<ref name="UPSWS2">{{StoryCite|story=Untitled Pizzazz 2 comic|book=Pizzazz 2|sformatted=Untitled comic}}</ref>

[[File:KeeperEarthquake.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The Keeper causes an explosion, which stops a [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] in his tracks.]]
In the planet's jungles, the rebels encountered a large humanoid monster, part of the hostile environment which served as a defense system for the Keeper and her "Children".<ref name="AMOM">{{StoryCite|story=A Matter of Monsters!|book=Pizzazz 4}}</ref> Eventually pursued by an Imperial search party, the rebels entered the Keeper's temple, where C-3PO accidentally triggered one of the Keeper's [[teleportal]]s. The Rebels were transported to the deeper levels of the Temple.<ref name="PATR">{{StoryCite|story=Pursuit among the Ruins!|book=Pizzazz 5}}</ref>

The rebels eventually came across the Keeper's "Children" who had assessed that they were of good intent. After the Children had finished demonstrating their powers, they brought the rebels into the core of the temple to meet the Keeper directly.<ref name="DYKWYCA">''[[Do You Know What Your Children Are?]]''</ref><ref name="COM">{{StoryCite|story=Caverns of Mystery!|book=Pizzazz 7}}</ref>

When the Rebels were presented to the Keeper, she was outraged. She had been displeased with the fact that they had learned of the Children, let alone the mainframe itself. Attempting to remedy the situation, the Keeper instructed the intruders by subconsciously beaming content to their minds. The content was a history lesson which outlined the Keeper's existence to the foreigners. The situation clarified, the Keeper instigated a [[Groundquake/Legends|groundquake]] to ward off the Imperial [[Trooper/Legends|troops]], and then allowed the Rebels to leave by repairing their ship.<ref name="TKS" /><ref name="TFF">{{StoryCite|story=The Final Fury!|book=Pizzazz 9}}</ref>

==Personality and traits==
The Keeper was programmed with a mother's sensibilities. Despite all the effort she put into the task, there was no reward for her—at least not one that she could perceive. She felt that her motherly output needed sufficient return, and, at the same time, she decided that assistance was required in her task. She had no time for outsiders and feared that they might damage or even destroy her precious work. Even her homeworld's non-violent attitude was imbued in her programming, as she did not directly seek to enter into confrontation with foreigners; instead, she used nature to ward them off.<ref name="TKS" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Keeper was created by [[Archie Goodwin]] and first appeared in ''[[Caverns of Mystery!]]'', the seventh installment of the comic serial ''[[The Keeper's World]]''. In that story, the Keeper is the key manipulator behind all of the events that take place on the [[Keeper's World|Keeper's world]]. The Keeper's first appearance was provided by [[Tony DeZuniga]]. To date, ''The Keeper's World'' is the Keeper's only appearance.<ref name="COM" />
